title: Ivesia beneolens
intro: Ivesia beneolens, a plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, is a rare and endemic species found in the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been traditionally used by the indigenous Maori people for its medicinal properties, including its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Ivesia beneolens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Ivesia beneolens is often prepared as a tincture or infused oil, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
